title: Looking for a volunteer to help a food poverty non-profit
excerpt: Hi there, I thought it was worth asking here because here is where all the Monday experts are!  I have tried advertising elsewhere but I guess Monday skills are pretty specialist and so we haven’t found anyone :frowning: …

posts.author: AliciaWeston (Alicia Weston)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there, I thought it was worth asking here because here is where all the Monday experts are! I have tried advertising elsewhere but I guess Monday skills are pretty specialist and so we haven’t found anyone

Bags of Taste is the leading food skills educator in the UK. We work with highly vulnerable people with mental and physical health challenges, poverty, and other difficult life circumstances to improve what they eat and their finances. We’re incredibly effective and after our 2-week programme people have less reliance on food banks, their health is improving, and they’ve more money in their pockets.

Ken is a recovering alcoholic who did our programme and it has helped keep him away from takeaways and convenience shops where he might be tempted to buy alcohol. Lisa, a single parent with chronic depression, did our programme along with her kids and now they’re no longer eating just pizza, chicken nuggets and chips, but making their own pasta sauces and experimenting with new foods.

We work with 2,500 people like Ken and Lisa every year and are set to grow - which is why we had to migrate off google sheets and onto Monday to track our participants and keep them safe.

We have been migrating our beneficiary tracking systems to Monday and developing our new system, we’re basically rolling out the system now but there are a still a few challenging bugs that we could do with help with from a specialist.

We have some extra bells and whistles we’d like on the system, which could be a small project for someone.

We also need some very low level, long term support - someone to call when we don’t know how to do something, or that can make small changes like if we have a new data collection field.

Can you help? I would love to hear from you if you can. Bags of Taste is such an amazingly positive organisation and your input can help us support the next 2,500 people.

To see more about our impact, please look at this 4 min video: https://youtu.be/DUgEFXMMCL4
or see www.bagsoftaste.org

posts.author: AliciaWeston (Alicia Weston)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there, I’m still looking for someone that is able to help us with this, in case there’s anyone out there?

posts.author: Tom-CXmonday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @AliciaWeston, Tom from monday.com Really excited to hear you decided to use our platform for your organization, and would love to help however I can!

Have you tried contacting our support team already? We have a really big team of experts available 24/7, so if you haven’t yet, I really recommend reaching out to them through the in-app chat and consulting them about everything you need help with.

With that said, if you need a more tailored support system or want to have an expert build workflows for you, you can also search for a monday Partner services here: https://monday.com/p/find-a-partner/

Other than that, I can also recommend trying out the AI tools around the platform that help build boards and workflows, like monday vibe, monday magic, the Sidekick…

And of course, if you have something specific you’d like to bring up here, please do!

posts.author: AshcroftJewellery (Christian Fox )
posts.cookedHtml: Dropped you a message through your website

posts.author: AliciaWeston (Alicia Weston)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Christian, so sorry but we don’t seem to have any message from you? You can message me on alicia@bagsoftaste.org

posts.author: AliciaWeston (Alicia Weston)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Tom, thank you for this, I do believe my IT volunteer has been in touch with the support team and they’ve been helpful, but I think what we need is a bit beyond a general support team and more specific . We tried a paid-for service and found they simply didn’t care about a little organisation like us, we were always an afterthought as we will never be a huge client. Thank you though.

posts.author: Tom-CXmonday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml:
@AliciaWeston Sorry to hear about this experience

I just messaged you here, will be happy to continue discussing in the messages and see if there’s another way we can help.

posts.author: AliciaWeston (Alicia Weston)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone, unfortunately I’m still looking for someone to help us. If there’s anyone out that that wants to put their Monday skills to good use helping make a serious difference in the world, please do let me know.
Here’s a recent example of the outcome of our work:

Chloe was referred by her social worker as she had depression which was really affecting her.

Tasks such as shopping, deciding what to cook and preparing food were overwhelming.

She was spending £200 a month on ready meals and takeaways.

Since completing, Chloe has started creating simple weekly meal plans and incorporating more frozen vegetables now that she knows they’re nutritious.

Her confidence in following recipes and experimenting has increased, and improved cooking skills and budgeting have lowered her weekly expenditure by £25.

The flexible structure of the programme and group support has helped her to get a new routine which suits her schedule and energy levels. Chloe says she has now found enjoyment in cooking.

She’ll continue to plan meals and use batch-cooking techniques so she has healthy, home-cooked options readily available.
